evidence.scientific_clarity: Violin+box+jitter for all 4 conditions in correct order (Control→low→high→Combo), significance brackets vs Control, dashed reference line at 1.0 — brief fully satisfied.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 never set; PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as Type3 (bitmap, non-selectable); default DejaVu Sans used throughout with no explicit rcParams font override.
evidence.palette: Seaborn defaults: green (#55A868) assigned to Drug-A 10 µM and red (#C44E52) to Drug-A 50 µM — categorical dose conditions, not directional; red-green pair is problematic for deuteranopia/protanopia.
evidence.layout_density: 6.5×5 in with tight_layout; violin widths 0.6, box widths 0.18, jitter ±0.22 — space used efficiently; significance brackets add vertical room without crowding; no overlapping labels.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes all visual elements and matches the figure; figure title is redundant with caption; no specific numerical result (e.g., median reduction %) stated anywhere.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF confirmed to contain /Subtype /Type3 DejaVu Sans — text is not selectable or editable; script never calls matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype'] = 42.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (29 KB), figure.png (73 KB), caption.tex (446 B), gen_figure.py (4.3 KB) all present and non-trivial; bonus figure.svg also produced.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains KDE violins, IQR boxes, median line, jitter alpha, reference line, and significance notation — but reports no concrete numerical finding (e.g., 'Combo reduced median viability to X').

overall_strengths: Complete visual encoding: violin + box + jitter + significance brackets + reference line all implemented correctly per brief, with correct condition ordering.
All required output files present and non-trivial; tight_layout and clean spine removal give a publication-ready layout.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 not set — PDF text is Type3 (bitmap), making it non-editable in Illustrator/Inkscape and likely to trigger journal rejection.
Red-green palette assignment to non-directional dose conditions is colorblind-unsafe and violates directional color discipline; no numerical finding in caption weakens scientific communication.
